AMD.reasoning: Vote count: 0 bullish / 1 bearish / 4 neutral. Unprecedented unanimity against entering a new long at current prices ($354-357), despite AMD's underlying trend being one of the strongest on the watchlist (daily ADX 74.94, 21d ROC +74.26%). The bull thesis is real — AI capex cycle intact, Lisa Su execution, DA Davidson upgrade, macro regime risk-on — but every agent independently reached the same timing conclusion: this is not an entry point. The lone directional call is swing_mean_reversion at bearish/60 confidence, fading the statistical extreme (Z-score 2.6, RSI 83-86, Bollinger %B 0.93, price 52% above 50-SMA, 24% above 21 EMA). Three agents (trend_momentum, breakout, catalyst_news) are formally neutral and waiting. Macro_context is neutral with a pass verdict driven by R/R math: at $357 entry, the upside to Fib 127.2% extension ($399) is only 11.8% vs ~7.8% downside to hourly support ($328.81), producing a 1.51:1 R/R — below the 2:1 minimum threshold required by strategy rules. The earnings binary on May 5 (3-4 trading days away) is a capital preservation veto that all five agents independently cited — entering a swing position ahead of an undefined earnings gap on a stock already at statistical extremes is not a swing trade, it is a binary bet. Wiki memory reinforces the pass: the AMD wiki shows a prior run (April 17) correctly avoided chasing at RSI 91 with a tight limit entry that was never filled — the system has good discipline on this name. The lessons page adds a direct parallel: the NVDA EMA-pullback setup in RSI 81-87 territory stopped out at -$264.65 on April 30, the same RSI range AMD is in today. That is the only closed trade in the lessons log, and it flags the exact setup profile AMD presents. The correct action is hold-cash and monitor. Two re-entry paths exist: (1) post-earnings base formation after May 5 — if AMD gaps up and holds, a breakout entry above the gap-up level with volume confirmation becomes valid; if AMD sells off, the $308-$315 zone (wiki consensus from multiple prior runs) offers a pullback-to-EMA re-entry with 3:1+ R/R. (2) Pre-earnings: any organic pullback to the 10 EMA (~$316) or 21 EMA (~$286) before May 5 would represent a genuine setup, but this requires price to retrace 10-20% in 3-4 days — unlikely without a macro shock.
AMD.key_conflicts: The primary conflict is swing_mean_reversion (bearish, 60 confidence, recommends shorting into the extreme at $354 with a $360 stop) versus swing_trend_momentum (neutral, explicitly warns against fading a daily ADX-74.94 trend). This is the textbook 'trend extended, reversal risk rising' disagreement the prompt anticipates. Trend_momentum is correct that the underlying force is extraordinary — ADX 74.94 with +DI 55.37 vs -DI 10.1 is not a trend to casually short. Mean_reversion is correct that the statistical extension (Z-score 2.6, RSI 83+, 52% above SMA-50) creates asymmetric snapback risk. Resolution: in a trending market context, swing_trend_momentum carries more weight, which means the short-fade thesis gets partial credit but not a full conviction call — the mean_reversion bearish signal is demoted to 'caution flag' rather than 'actionable trade.' The earnings binary on May 5 breaks the tie in favor of neutrality: it makes both a fresh long AND a short inadvisable because the gap risk is undefined in both directions. A secondary conflict exists between swing_breakout (which sees a technically valid $352.99 breakout trigger and a $396 measured-move target) and all other agents — breakout flags volume disqualification (only 1.05x vs required 1.5x) as the gating factor, so this is not a conflict about direction but about confirmation. Breakout would flip bullish on AMD post-earnings if volume confirms. No macro veto is needed here because swing_macro_context is already neutral rather than bearish — the macro supports the bull thesis long-term but does not override the timing concerns.
